reviews.text: The best ever!!

My journey began over 3 years ago with this firm . I was introduced to Christine Piti . I was involved in a car accident and was not sure what to do . Right at the beginning she showed compassion towards my injuries as well as directing me on what to do next . There was never a time where I was rushed into any decisions but she always showed concern to my injuries and always told me quote … “Just get better and take as much time as you need for it “ . We spoke occasionally when I needed advice or to just give her an update but there was never a push to hurry up , she only gave advice that I needed and always reassured me not to worry about anything . After a year and a half of therapy and surgery again when we spoke her only words were to get better , don’t worry about anything and assured me that everything would be ok . The issues dragged on for almost 3 years due to the injuries sustained in the accident but I never worried because she always settled me down . I manage a very large business with many employees that work for me and I would give anything to have a person like Christine on my team . Her personality was always reassuring . She always showed compassion towards me the client and my injuries . I would highly recommend this firm to anyone out there as well as giving them a perfect 5 or a perfect 10 . Whatever the highest number is , her and the firm deserve it . I had zero issues dealing with with her and always after speaking with her had a reassurance that they were doing what was best for me their client
Thank you Christine
reviews.ownerResponse: Jim. We appreciate your vote of approval. Thankful to know you’re feeling better. We are here if you ever need us.

My path towards citizenship was hugely augmented by Attorney Claudia Grégoire and her warm, friendly legal assistants and staff. The process of filing the application, getting all the details right IS unfamiliar and scary. Even when an applicant has no obvious obstacles blocking their path, it’s scary. It feels like being a new kid on the first day of school. But Andy (whom I met a couple of times) was just so commonsensical and patient! It was really, REALLY great to meet her. I didn’t meet Anllely, but she was friendly and helpful in her communications. And then, at the time of the final interview, to have Janne Reisch alongside me was absolutely fabulous. Every little bit made a huge difference. Tomorrow is the Oath Taking ceremony – and I’m STILL NERVOUS. Because changing one’s citizenship is a big deal. I hope to come back here to post one more note, this time with a photograph to show that it has really happened. With warm, sincere thanks.

I can’t thank the Law Office of Claudia Grégoire enough for guiding me through every step of my immigration journey, and Andy Mitchell — from my marriage petition all the way to securing asylum. From day one, Claudia and her team were knowledgeable, organized, and incredibly compassionate.

What stood out most was Claudia’s personal commitment — she treated my case with the same care and urgency as if it were her own family’s. Thanks to her expertise and dedication, I felt supported and confident at every stage.

If you are looking for an immigration attorney who combines deep legal expertise with kindness and integrity, I highly recommend Claudia Grégoire and her amazing team. They truly changed my life.

Dennis and Aidan

I had an excellent experience with Ed Formisano and his team. He resolved a difficult situation for me and was encouraging and professional throughout. I would absolutely refer Formisano & Company to anyone - especially if you are looking for a compassionate and hard working team that will have your back. Thank you Ed, Diane and everyone on the team who supported me through a difficult and ultimately successful legal issue.

I do not recommend, I hired Edward to represent me with an employment issue, he started off bold and strong, at this time I felt great with my decision in hiring him.  He served my employer with a letter, putting him on notice, stating " Please advise me if you have interest in engaging in a dialogue regarding  ....." When my employer contacted Edward to discuss, Edward told him that he could not converse with him, he could only speak about the matter with his lawyer (which he did not have).  He then suggested to my employer hiring his good friend, which my employer did.  From that moment on Edward completely backed down, his responses completely contradicted themselves, usually I was answered with "Ummmm" and "Uhhh".  Every conversation I was left with more questions then I even started with.  He likes to keep all communication on the phone, which means no documentation of the conversation, I quickly stopped allowing phone conversations and made all communication happen through email.  Email responses from him were VERY short.  Closing the entire negotiation was a nightmare, I did not allow Edward to go with me to sign the final documents.  My retainer which I willingly paid was $5000, not sure if he thought that this was going to be a quick score but I can not fathom that it is ethically right to tell someone "I can only speak with your lawyer", to then be told that they do not have one, then suggesting your good friend to represent them.  Once the other attorney was hired, Edward completely backed down.  In the state of Rhode Island you can absolutely self represent (if my employer wanted to).  Again, I do not recommend, and I think a lot of the reviews posted are fake.
My review of Edward does not include his staff, Diane in the office is fabulous and I appreciate her quick communication always.

I was deeply impressed by the level of expertise Mr. Formisano brought to our conversations. More importantly, he was kind and compassionate throughout a difficult situation, and he was careful to explain each step of the process along the way.

Ed was recommended to me by a family member, and I’m so glad that I contacted him.

I had never needed legal help and wasn’t sure where to start, but Ed was extremely knowledgeable and reassuring regarding my situation. His secretary was also lovely to chat with. Both were genuinely compassionate and a pleasure to work with, which was not the experience I was expecting to have.

Hopefully I don’t need Ed again in the future, but if I do, I won’t hesitate to reach out.

In the past I used this attorney for my daughters case and we had another attorney in the office on the case she was great and very helpful so I didn’t search anywhere else when I needed one for myself recently I went with formisamo law a 2nd time I was told I’d get a call to go over my case either the day before or morning of I never got that call was totally unprepared for my hearing. He did represent me at my hearing, but he interviewed me prior. He could’ve asked better questions. Of myself the claimant and the defendant I am still awaiting a decision so I’m not sure yet if we’ve won but I never received a call from them after the hearing either he even went as far as to say he didn’t have any more time to question the defendant because he has to go to court Last time I checked, I was as good of a paying customer as anyone else I booked my hearing over a month in advance And if you didn’t have time to take my case, you didn’t have to it was totally unprofessional and I will not be calling them in the future

After losing my job unexpectedly, I really didn’t know what I was going to do. I called Ed Formisano, and his assistant, Diane, answered the phone.  I explained my circumstances to her, and she couldn’t have been more sympathetic or compassionate, and assured me that Ed would take good care of me.  Ed spoke with me later the same day, and I agreed to meet with him in person the following day.  We came up with a game plan, and Ed took it from there.  Ed and Diane were in constant communication with me during the process, and whenever I contacted them, I received an e-mail or phone response within 24 hours. Ed was able to negotiate a severance agreement that was much more appropriate than what had been originally offered. I would highly recommend Formisano and Company if you are having any employment issues that require legal attention. Ed and Diane are completely knowledgeable in this area, and will see that you receive the best compensation possible, according to your circumstances. I would happily use them again if the need ever arises.

I had the pleasure of working with Ed Formisano on my employment case. Ed was unlike most attorneys I have met. He was very knowledgeable about employment law but he was also genuinely empathetic regarding my feelings and what I was going through during this extremely stressful period. He was successful in obtaining a reasonable judgment without a court hearing and therefore limiting added trauma. Another absolute pleasure of Formisano Law office is Diane. When you call the office the first person you speak to is Diane. She has a warm, caring and supportive disposition and is always there with a kind word or an answer if you have any concerns. She made the last few months much easier with a phone call or a simple email to explain what was happening. I would recommend Ed to all my friends and family members as an attorney for any work related litigation. Thank you again Ed and Diane for all that you do. You have both succeeded in making my life a little bit easier to smile today!

reviews.text: Only had a phone call and a ton-TON- of ton of tonedeaf txts from "Thierry" that i eventually had to block. Have had debilitating health issues for years, and got a new disease ( sarcoidosis) and pacemaker/ ICD in feb 25, that has made my day to day life extremely difficult. My therapist suggested looking into disability- I got a call/ voicemail orginally from a male lawyer, I called the number he gave in the voicemail and was met with the most rude secretary, who was so callous, anyways she told me i had to call the local SS office to find out my date last insured. I call the SS office and the person I talked to was more helpful but told me that finding out the date last insured is something the law firm should be doing and not me. After that, they kept calling everyday-EVERYDAY- and not leaving messages, and they never emailed me, which is what i orginally requested to avoid situations like this that DO NOT HELP MY HEALTH!! Thanks- theres more lawyers out there- avoid this law firm at all cost!

Before I signed with this firm, they called me every day, emailed me, and texted me. They said, "We're going to work on your case. We'll consult with our partner to see if there's a conflict of interest." They evaluated and reviewed my case. Then, total silence for about two months. They contacted me, but they haven't received my records. I'm a patient who is allergic to chlorine. I've had allergy tests that have come back positive since I was a child. Now, I can't have chlorine in my house. I have to bathe with special filters; I can't swim in a pool. At the Rhode Island hospital, on more than one occasion—around five times—they used chlorine on me, which led to me being intubated for several weeks. Not once or twice, but five times. I was intubated because of what I did. Their protocol included a sign saying I was allergic to [unclear - possibly "Breathe-Schung" or "Sn" in some other contexts]. And yet, I kept going back. I contacted this law firm, and they said, "We're going to take your case. We have your record." For four months, I had absolutely no communication with the law firm. Nothing. They said they were reviewing my case. The last thing they told me was that I would contact partner Mari, the notary, and give you an answer in a week. They said, "Give me a week to work on getting back to you about what we're going to do." After that week, they contacted me via email with a simple "they can't take my case," saying that if we don't go, we have to prove I'm allergic. But the allergy test is right there, the evidence of how many times I was intubated, the doctors' written records explaining why I was intubated. That was it. So I started investigating, looking into the hospital's lawyer, and with the people I had met with at the beginning, the hospital's representatives happen to be study partners of several lawyers at this firm, which is kind of strange, I think. So I had to go to Boston to have the case evaluated there to take action because it seems odd that for more than four months they tell me, "Yes, we'll work at your house," and then suddenly overnight, "Oh, we can't work with you." In this case, they haven't even wanted to return my record, nothing. They've left me in limbo, like they're trying to stab me in the back, not knowing that I could be hospitalized for several months, more than three months in the hospital. Now I need to use oxygen, I can't work. All of this is happening at home. I'm 36 years old, I have two daughters. I didn't ask to be unable to work. I went to a hospital to seek help. I went to a hospital because I had a condition, where it's supposed to be the safest place. The Rhode Island hospital put my life at risk more than five times. I can't work because of the oxygen deficiency in my lungs, the damage caused by so many intubations. My body has been severely affected, and this law firm tells me it's difficult because if we go to court, we have to find an expert who can prove that, and it's very, very difficult to prove. Meanwhile, I have to suffer like this, and they call themselves fighting for the health of patients.

We are focused on analyzing your case and welcome the opportunity to continue addressing any questions directly.

This review is SOLEY for Attorney Maryann Violette and her immediate support staff.  I hired Maryann as my representative for an SSI Claim in April 2023.  From the beginning Maryann was very personable, though very scatter brained and came across as very unorganized.

Typically I do try and find the positives when first meeting someone and especially someone that will be helping me.  When you hire an Attorney, you remember much more of the interactions than the Attorney themselves, simply due to you only have one Attorney and they potentially have hundreds.

Throughout our conversations while applying for the SSI claim Maryann was very optimistic and stated many times that I had a very strong case.  She knew and I reminded her (sent files) that I am a 100% Combat Veteran, so my case should be fast tracked, according to the SSA Website.  This was made very clear to her.

After several months of no movement, I contacted her to ensure that she did in fact set up my claim to be fast tracked.  This is where the true colors starting to show.  She seemed very confused, partially blaming her staff and unsure if she got a confirmation.  Maryann routinely would state on both scheduled and unscheduled calls that she "didn't have my file in front of her", "couldn't see the screens she needed", "my assistant should have done that".  This was beyond concerning and frustrating, again as her being my only Lawyer I remembered very specific things about our talks.  Emails to her went unanswered for many days, sometimes NEVER responded to at all.  She seemed not to understand her inbox and sent box, constantly blaming her computer.

After approximately 11 months I received a notification that my claim was denied and shortly thereafter my written decision was sent.  I was able to see, for the first time how she completed my claim forms.  The forms were littered with inaccuracies and misspellings/fragmented sentences.  When we spoke on the phone that day, she began saying "well they always deny the initial claim".  I called her out on this was a total 180 on her initial statements and statements over the past 11 months.  She then said to file your appeal I want your Doc to fill out a form.  She had no contact information.  I provided a detailed email with his LinkedIn page, phone number and direct email address.  I received no response for 2 days, so I followed up on the phone to confirm she got it.  She again was confused and said "you never sent it to me".  I had already become very disappointed with my representation and the outlook if I continued with her.  After sometime on the phone with me, she found the email and said SHE would have the form sent to him and we set a conference call for this past Tuesday at 1:30am.  That gave her well over 2 weeks to get it sent.  When that call came, immediately realized she was unprepared with this call and began deflecting when I asked if she had the form from my Doc.  She said I don't have it here, I can't see your file (we had a schedule call!!) and I have more screens at home than in my Office.  I was angry at this point and asked if she even sent the forms, she deflected and put it on her assistant.  Again, over 2 weeks of time to get this done and this is where we are.  She then got very flustered and put me on hold, 15 minutes later hung up.  During that hold time I contacted my Doc on the VA Messaging app.  He confirmed that NOTHING was sent via email or US Mail.  I decided she was done as my representative.  I called back and was told she would call me back.  I stated that this was a one way call, she needed to be told that she is fired.  Magically I was connected.  Maryann began arguing before I could speak and I told her that I needed to say one thing to her, that this would be a one way call.  She said "Bye!" and hung up on me.

A written and formal complaint was sent to the Rhode Island Supreme Court Discipline Board.  I highly cautious anyone using her services.
Very professional and caring and worked very hard to complete my WC case!

I got rear ended in 2015 at a red light which resulted in my chest breaking my steering wheel and an annular tear in my lower back. I had initially signed on another popular tv ad law firm.... BIG MISTAKE. Great attorneys do not need catch phrases and silly commercials. Every time I called I spoke to a new person, I had to do most of the leg work in acquiring records, and I never once sat with or met an actual attorney.

I attended a Lawyers Guild gathering with a friend who is also an attorney. At this gathering I met three different attorneys who did not know each other, who recommended Attorney Paul Cantor as being the best in the personal injury business.

Boy were they right! I pulled my file from the Other law firm, and I was immediately impressed with him and his staff. I actually sat down with him and his paralegal Tracy, and went over my case in detail. They listened to me and took every bit of information seriously, which was also very well documented. Every single inquiry I had was answered promptly, and Atty Cantor was available to advise on every occasion that arose. I felt like I was in great hands from start to finish, and I am happy to say he settled my case successfully! After signing on with the law office when I met with Ron, I was represented by another lawyer the day of my virtual nominal divorce hearing. This lawyer was surprised to see me in-person at the law office for the hearing despite my letting he & Ron know days prior that I would be there. He then spent almost the majority of the hearing on his phone, scrolling through Facebook and had a very callous attitude towards the whole situation (there were also mice droppings in his office). At the end of the hearing I was told that all paperwork would be submitted and that after the 3 months and 1 day waiting period my divorce would be final. I trusted that the law office would handle my case appropriately. I was wrong. By the end of November when the judiciary portal did not show that my divorce was final, I emailed and called the law office only to be told by a paralegal that the lawyer who represented me in court no longer worked at the agency and failed to submit the paperwork so the divorce could not be finalized without another hearing to file "out of time". The paralegal even went so far as to ask me what the color of the folder and notebook the lawyer used on the day of the hearing was so she could try to find any notes he took (he took none). I was floored. All and all 4 lawyers from the office "represented" me to the court (2 of whom I never met) and the one court hearing turned into three because of the incompetence of the lawyer they assigned to me for the nominal hearing (& the misunderstanding of the 'filing out of time' process by the law office). I am now officially divorced, 6 months later than I should have been, and the icing on the cake is that the letter I was sent by the law office with a copy of my final judgement was addressed to another person. These are just some of the ridiculous things I experienced when dealing with this law firm. Save your money and your time and find another law office with competent staff.
*Edited to add in response to Ron's reply - I was not informed of the original lawyer's hospitalization and medical issue until I called the office in November to inquire about my case finalization. I was not informed in a timely manner.
reviews.ownerResponse: I am sorry you were not happy with the service we provided to you. However you were informed that the original lawyer who handled your case had been hospitalized with medical issues and never returned to work at my office. You were assigned a new attorney who finished the process and communicated with you regularly by email and answered  your questions promptly to my knowledge. The case was finalized and you were sent a copy of the final paperwork the same day it was available by both email and mail. Good luck in the future.
Thank you Raphael. You have been a great client.
Thank you, Albert, for your excellent rating. Your support is invaluable to Ron Bonin Law, and we appreciate your trust in our legal services here in Cranston.

reviews.ownerResponse: Joe, you contacted our office regarding a matter. Once we reviewed the video we returned it to the address you provided along with a letter informing you we could no longer represent you. You then contacted us after the letter was sent demanding we send you the video.  We then burned another cd, and sent it to you.  Yes I did hang up the phone on you because you were being belligerent to staff, and I refuse to let anyone degrade my staff.  I then took the call and you continued, so I ended the call, you were no longer a client and I do not need to subject myself to listening to your ramblings and wild accusations.  That being said I wish nothing but the best for you, sincerely.  May you find peace in your life.

I’m sharing my experience with Sinapi Law in Warwick, RI, so others can make an informed decision. More than five years ago, I hired the firm for a matter in which attorney Gregory Mancini was involved. At the time, to my understanding, he also held a town-council role. In my view, this created serious concerns about conflicts of interest and judgment.
After that matter, I pursued a separate case on my own. From my perspective, communications from Sinapi/Mr. Mancini became unhelpful and adversarial. Requests for my complete client file went unanswered or were met with vague replies, and I did not receive a clear explanation addressing my concerns. I also received messages that, to me, felt confusing and harassing, questioning my identity rather than resolving the underlying records issue.
To be clear, this review reflects my personal account and opinions. However, based on what I experienced—poor communication, lack of transparency about records, and what I perceived as retaliatory behavior tied to outside relationships—I cannot recommend this firm or Mr. Mancini. If you’re considering them, I strongly suggest you ask detailed conflict-of-interest questions, get commitments in writing about records access, and consult independent references before retaining counsel.
I hope this helps others avoid the frustration I went through.

I recently turned to Sinapi Law for some employment-related questions, and I was so impressed with their quick, responsive, and professional service. It was clear from the start how much they genuinely cared about helping me. Wonderful team of people, Richard, Danilo, and Lori. I’m very grateful for their guidance and highly recommend them to anyone looking for thoughtful and reliable legal support.

I had reached out to Karen last year. We talked on the phone and I immediately liked her. I was asked to sign a form agreeing to retain her and sent in some of my paperwork. I didnt here anything back so I emailed her again and still no reply. At that time it was close to the holidays so I figured Id wait till after to pursue anything. I let it drag out longer than necessary and was in a really bad spot. Thinking back to our conversation I decided to reach out again. I spoke with her legal assistant and set up an appointment. I was a nervous wreck going in, but finally felt hopeful. Again I signed the contract, gave my financial information only to get a call next day telling me they weren't going to work with me stating she her case load was to high to take my case. I felt defeated. I felt angry. I drove 45 mins there and back. Now I had to do the same drive to obtain all my paperwork which they kept at the meeting the day before. I had to take time off of work to get this all done so I lost not just time but money and I now I am back at square one. I understand that you want to get a feel of the case, but that could have been done over the phone and if your case load is already high, why bother having the appointment and keeping my documents only for me to come back.
Upon entering the office no one even greets you either. I waited 10 mins in the lobby before walking down the hallway to find them just sitting in the office after buzzing me in.
Very disappointed in the whole experience.

Karen is an absolute pleasure to work with. She takes the time to explain every step clearly and ensures you understand the entire process. Karen is top tier - she is incredibly intelligent, professional, but is ready to drop the gloves and fight for her clients. There's no one else I'd rather have in my corner than Karen, especially when it comes to the intricacies of a contentious divorce. reviews.text: Waste of time after comparing them with two other firms who already want my case, they spent almost 90 minutes getting info and documents from me only to decline without explanation. As others have said, they cite "legal reasons" for not disclosing their decision to decline, however I have dealt with counsel privately and professionally for many years and have always received an explanation when representation is declined. Again as others have said in their reviews, it does not seem like they are interested in assisting with complex VA disability cases. 2 stars for the total lack of transparency in their process but the woman who did my intake work was excellent to speak with and very thorough. Clearly if two other smaller firms want my case they believe they can win, so I was very surprised to be declined and have them refuse to explain why.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We understand how frustrating it can feel to invest significant time in an intake process and then learn that we are unable to move forward with representation.

Our team conducts thorough evaluations so we can make careful, ethically guided decisions about the cases we accept. When we decline representation, we are often limited in what we can share regarding the specific reasons, consistent with professional obligations and firm policy. We recognize that this can feel unsatisfying, and we appreciate your perspective on transparency.

We’re glad to hear that your experience with our intake team member was positive, and we will be sure to share your kind words. We sincerely wish you the very best as you move forward with the firm you choose and in pursuing the benefits you believe you are entitled to.

reviews.text: CC&K is an excellent law firm. Reliable immensely knowledgeable professionals. The staff is open and honest guiding the veteran through the processes with success for the veteran to receive the correct disability rating.  The veterans concerns are their concerns. A trustworthy law firm. They respect the veterans and the veterans rights to receive a fair assessment of his or hers disabilities. This law firm is committed to ensuring the veteran is represented fully to the extent of the law by which the VA uses as their template for reviewing cases and awarding ratings. CC&K is the best law firm bar none. Thank you and wishing you continued success
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Greg, for taking the time to share such a thoughtful and detailed review. We truly appreciate your kind words and your trust in CCK Law. Advocating for veterans and ensuring they receive fair, accurate disability ratings is at the core of our mission, and it means a great deal to know that our commitment, transparency, and respect for veterans’ rights made a positive difference in your experience. On behalf of our attorneys and staff, thank you for your support and well wishes. It has been an honor to serve you, and we wish you continued success as well.

reviews.text: When it was all said and done I actually lost 150 a month because of this company
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for sharing your feedback. VA disability ratings are determined solely by the Department of Veterans Affairs based on the medical and legal evidence available at the time of review. In some cases, if VA determines that a condition has improved, it may reduce a rating, which is outside of our control. Our role is to advocate based on the evidence and applicable law, but the final decision always rests with VA. We appreciate you taking the time to share your perspective.
John, thank you for taking the time to leave a five-star rating. Your support means a lot to our team. We appreciate your trust and wish you all the best.

reviews.text: I'm an Army Ranger veteran (GWOT). After just a ten-minute phone call, I quickly realized they're greedy and wanted to siphon as much as they could off of me. I didn't even bother sending them my paperwork. They called and texted me a few times, I told them I wasn't interested, and then THEY sent ME an email saying they couldn't take on my case. Ha! I wouldn't touch them with a ten-foot pole. If you can find other options, don't bother with this one.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your experience, and we appreciate your service. We’re sorry to hear that your initial interaction left a negative impression. Our intake calls are intended to briefly understand a veteran’s situation and determine whether our firm is able to assist, and there are times when we ultimately decide not to proceed after that review. Any follow-up communications are meant to ensure clarity and closure in that process, not to be intrusive.

We respect your decision and regret any frustration this caused. We wish you well.

reviews.text:
I can’t remember the last time someone that had a future payday from me on the horizon was so blatantly rude to me at the onset.
Text saying we tried to reach you, but no missed calls. Already suspicious. So you call them. Agent dives right into personal questions before they will discuss anything about the process, fees, timeline, etc. You’ll need to have specific documentation ready to read and submit to them, and if you do not they will give you an incorrect method to obtain it. They will mention a denial letter multiple times after you explain you are not appealing a decision but amending a current one for a potential increase. Too fast through their script - they don’t even pay attention, they just read the next question.
20 minutes later they’ll tell you someone will review and get back to you. Very rude, dismissive, and unprofessional. Find someone better.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We’re sorry to hear that your initial interaction left a negative impression.

Our firm focuses on assisting veterans and their families after VA claims have been denied, and our intake process is structured to collect certain information up front so we can determine whether and how we may be able to help. This includes asking specific questions and reviewing documentation early in the conversation.

For more than 25 years, we’ve been honored to serve thousands of veterans and their families nationwide, and we’re proud of our record of advocating for veterans through meaningful, precedential cases. While not every inquiry ultimately results in representation, we are committed to treating each individual with professionalism and respect.

We wish you all the best going forward.

reviews.text:
This law firm only seems to want to help veterans with simple cases. Things a VSO would probably be able to handle for free.

Just like many other reviews, I submitted my information for review, and was told they would take all the easy stuff but the one thing I need an law firm for they wouldn't be able to help.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to share your perspective. We’re sorry to hear that your experience left you feeling disappointed.

Our firm carefully reviews every request for representation, and in some situations we may determine that we’re not the right fit based on the specific legal issues involved or the stage of a claim. That decision is never a reflection on the importance of a veteran’s concerns, but rather on whether we can responsibly and effectively assist.

We respect the vital role that VSOs play and believe veterans should work with whichever resource is best suited to their needs. We wish you the best.

reviews.text: This law firm denied my disability claim outright, but Hill & Ponton accepted it and achieved a 100% disabilty rating in less than two years. If you desire professional handling, a highly competent staff, compassion, expertise, and honest representation, choose the experts at Hill & Ponton.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your perspective. We’re sorry to hear that your experience with our firm did not meet your expectations.

While outcomes can differ based on the unique facts and evidence of each case, we respect every veteran’s decision to choose the representation they believe is best for them and are glad to hear you ultimately achieved a result you’re satisfied with. Our role is always to provide an honest assessment based on the record at the time and to act in accordance with applicable law and evidence.

We are proud of our long-standing work on behalf of veterans, including our record of handling precedential cases and helping tens of thousands of veterans navigate the VA claims and appeals process. That commitment continues to guide our approach in every matter we review. We wish you well.

reviews.text: Waste of space i asked for representation they refused ajd have refused to release my file preventing me from getting the nedical care I need they are evil incarnate
reviews.ownerResponse:
We strongly disagree with the characterization presented in this review.

At no point did our team refuse to release any records or obstruct anyone from seeking medical care. That allegation is both inaccurate and harmful. We take all client communications seriously and document interactions thoroughly.

We understand that being denied representation can be frustrating. This does not mean we view your case as unimportant. We remain committed to supporting veterans through our free educational resources, including our blog, YouTube channel, and tools like the VA Claim Builder.

reviews.text: Submitted my information to them to see about holding the VA accountable for understating the situation with my disabilities.  I was given an answer of they can't help me. I let it go. Then wondered why they couldn't help. So contacted them back. Asking for an explanation as to why they couldn't help me. Maybe there is something I can do to help my case. I was told legally they don't have to share why they can't.  The truth is if active claims are open. They won't touch the situation until decisions have been made. Which is fine. The claims open pertain to surgery so. The claims have been decided on. Now no claims in process. Because it was a big secret and legally can't tell me after I forwarded my personal information. I will not use them. Imagine giving personal information and being told there's a legal issue with telling you why they can't help you.  Amazing, I'm sure now that the other claims have completed. Now attorneys can help. Unfortunately it won't be CC&K. There was one that also contacted me and said if there are open claims they can help. Not sure what the big secret was that made it so I can't be helped by them. However another attorney said the same thing. We can't help. When asked why it was due to an open appeal and a request after surgery. Which go quickly. Should of been patient. But lost business because they're protecting a secret they legally can't state. Oh wrll.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for your feedback. We understand your frustration and regret that your experience left you feeling unsupported.

Submitting documentation for our review does not establish an attorney-client relationship. Our legal team evaluates many factors, including case posture and procedural stage, to determine whether we are in a position to help. If a claim is still active or pending a VA decision, we typically cannot intervene until it is complete, which is a common limitation across many veterans law firms.

We truly appreciate your consideration and hope you are able to find the support that best suits your needs.

reviews.text:
Total waste of time.  You talk to some random person on the phone who tells you that they will accept your case, then you spend an hour reviewing symptoms, discussing personal medical issues, sending them sensitive personal information and files and are left with the impression that they are going to help.  Days later you get an email saying " After a thoughtful review by our legal team, we determined that we won't be able to offer representation for this specific case. We want you to know that your situation is important to us, and we are here to support you. If there is another claim on your mind, our team is more than willing to explore it with you.
Should you need further assistance with this claim, we have provided the Disabled American Veteran's (DAV) number as a resource. We wish you the best."

The reality is that they probably actually could help, but they don't think its worth their time.  Maybe if you have a really old denial or something, but TBH it is a waste of time and then they just refer you to the DAV.

What a joke don't waste your time.  Surely they will post some BS reply under this.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We understand that it can be frustrating to go through the intake process and then learn we are unable to offer representation. Please know that our legal team thoroughly reviews each case before making a decision, and we take that responsibility seriously.

If we declined to take your case, it does not mean it lacks merit. Due to high veteran demand and limited capacity, we can only accept a certain number of cases. While we can’t provide legal advice or specific reasons for not moving forward, we strongly encourage veterans to continue seeking the benefits they’ve earned. Our VSOs, blog, YouTube channel, and free tools like the VA Claim Builder are available to support you.

We truly appreciate your service and wish you all the best moving forward.

He came to the correct conclusion that I didn’t have a case & even recommended getting a second opinion to be sure. Very thorough & knowledgeable. Pleasant & caring demeanor.

xperience.  Totally unethical and (need I say more) .
Brie DiLibero is an exceptional attorney. She is knowledgeable, intelligent and a fierce advocate for her clients. She is an exceptional litigator. I recommenced her highly.

reviews.text: I had a really positive experience here. Amanda and Nelson were helpful, knowledgeable, and easy to work with. They answered all my questions and kept everything moving smoothly. It’s clear they care about their clients.

My father was involved in a car accident last week and reached out to Robles Law for assistance. He was scheduled for an appointment yesterday (of last week) at a specific time, but no one was present upon his arrival. He was then instructed to return today at 11:00 AM to meet with the attorney; however, once again, no one was there on time, and the lawyer never arrived. He has now been asked to return tomorrow at noon.

This pattern of unprofessional behavior raises serious concerns about the firm’s reliability and commitment to clients. Additionally, we overheard staff members making derogatory remarks about a particular group of people — an unacceptable practice, especially given that this group represents a large portion of our local community.

We advise others to exercise caution when considering this law firm, as our experience suggests a lack of professionalism and unequal treatment of clients.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Shillary, I’m very sorry for the confusion and frustration surrounding your father’s initial appointments. I was present at the office for the first meeting and greeted your dad. Your dad ended up stepping out while we waited for my partner to arrive, didn't return, and didn't answer our follow-up calls that day. The next meeting was scheduled only with my partner, who was present at the office when your dad arrived. They called me together and asked that I come into the office for the meeting per your dad's request, so I drove in to meet with them but he again stepped out and didn't answer our calls. If we had known ahead of time he wanted to meet with both of us together, we would have been happy to accommodate that. Our procedure is to have one attorney present for our intakes, apologies for any misunderstandings. I understand how this situation was disappointing, and we truly value every client’s time and trust. We’d be happy to reconnect and make things right if he’s still interested in meeting.

Regarding your comment about remarks you allegedly overheard in our office, I do want to clarify that you weren’t present either day, and the only staff member in the office besides myself or my partner was the receptionist. She treats everyone with respect and would never allow or participate in that kind of behavior. We take professionalism and inclusivity very seriously and have zero tolerance for discrimination of any kind.

reviews.text:
We recently had a terrible experience with Brian Fogarty. He has been very elusive and unwilling to answer questions my brother and I have had for him. My mother made the mistake of hiring him before she passed and he seems to have no answers for any important questions. He took 10% of the value of my Mother's estate and I still haven't seen any documentation of what he did or even a copy of my Mother's will.  At probate court, he told the judge and us that we were the sole beneficiaries, yet someone else inhereted my mother's $20,000 vehicle. When we asked about it after the fact, he told us there must have been a special note about the vehicle. Must have been??? He also said that the executor declares the value of the estate! Wouldn't my mother have done that when she hired him? We both requested documents from her medical history because the state of RI took 25% of her estate. We both requested a copy of the will. It has been weeks and as of today we have received nothing that we requested! When I spoke to him on the phone he gave me the run around about every question I had. He kept saying I have to go! If I could give 0 Stars than I would have!

In regards to Brian's response, clearly I am not a client, I am the son of his client who passed away last year. When he says opposing side he's 100% lying as he was representing my family and my mother's will which are the same side. The opposing side was the state of Rhode Island, and considering my mother was paying him I would hope that he's not implying that he was on the side of the state of Rhode Island! Brian sent a letter to the state of Rhode Island contesting the fact that they were seeking 25k from my mother's estate from past medical expenses. Brian withdrew his contestation as soon as the state of RI sent him a packet of dates and amounts from 2015 to 2018. Brian failed to do his job as I later received a screenshot from my mother's partner regarding the medical billing. My mother's name was not even spelled correctly on the list, there's no social security number on there and he withdrew before even making a phone call to find out if they had the right person! Brian literally sat at the table with the Probate Judge, my brother, the executor, and myself and told the judge in front of all three of us that my brother and I were the only beneficiaries! My brother and I have a good relationship with my mother's partner and she was just as surprised by much of this as we were. My brother spoke with him on the phone before I did and he also said Brian would not take the time to explain anything to him. When he says opposing sides, he's either straight lying or does not know what he's talking about because he hasn't looked at the case! Finally, Brian is the one who hung up the phone on me because he didn't want to explain anything to me when I did call. That was probably because he had already taken his $7,700 and felt his job was done. I asked him how could I get a copy of the will to which he replied I could either go to City Hall or he could mail me one. I then asked if he could mail a copy to my brother and I to which he replied yes. Still haven't received it and now have to go to City Hall. I then said as far as the questions I have if you're not willing to take the time to explain things to me then maybe I will just have to have another attorney contact him. His response was go for it and then he disconnected!
reviews.ownerResponse: Complete disclosure.  He is not a client.  He is  on the opposite side of a case.  Called once after it had concluded and hung up.  That has been my only interaction with this person.

reviews.text:
Thomas Briody is an excellent lawyer and a very good representative of his client’s best interests.  When I first required Mr. Briody’s services he recognized that I needed help in more ways than just legal representation.  He put me in touch with services that I needed to regain my life.  He also provided me access to experts in the field of forensics and social work.  During that time he interacted with the Government on my behalf representing my best interests in argument after argument before the courts and behind the scenes.  He supported my need to continue my education and to regain my self esteem during the prolonged legal process.  In the end, I believe that Thomas was able to negotiate a fair and equitable agreement on my behalf with the courts.

Even after my interaction with the courts has ended, he is still involved with and caring of my needs as a client.  I have recommended his services to family friends who needed a good lawyer and advocate.  He has been highly effective in representing them as well.

I highly recommend his services to anyone who is in need of an excellent lawyer.

Sincerely,

David W. Norman, CPA, MBA

Tom Briody is God’s gift for anyone in need of an attorney. We knew no one in Rhode Island when we suddenly had need of legal services and found Tom on a website like this one. Based on his outstanding recommendations we decided to contact him. We called Tom on Monday morning and met for a consultation on the same day. Tom won our confidence in the initial consultation with his thorough and completely honest explanation of possible outcomes and how he wanted to handle the case. Tom is always very careful to tell you what could happen, what he will try to make happen and the likelihood of both. Tom is very conscientious with how he spends his time (your money). Tom hired a private investigator to begin gathering information and interviewing witnesses the same day we hired him. Tom skillfully built a solid defense based on facts and evidence and used it to successfully negotiate a favorable settlement with the district attorney. Tom is not only a great attorney but a good person as well. Tom’s sincere interest in my son’s wellbeing helped provide my son with the direction he needed to make positive changes in his life.  We are extremely grateful for Tom’s caring dedication, perseverance and intelligence.

Have no doubts when you hire Tom Briody to defend yourself or your family! You will get an honest, experienced and dedicated attorney! We hired Tom sight unseen so to speak. We knew nothing about him but he was recommended by an attorney in our home state and we needed an attorney right away. It turned out to be the best decision we could have made! He worked tirelessly with us for several years and we will always be grateful for his knowledge of the law and his hard work. He has years of experience, is extremely honest and very dedicated. He goes above and beyond in all that he does for his clients and, it is our fervent opinion that he was able to obtain the best outcome possible for us in a very high profile, high stress case. He is just a GREAT ATTORNEY and I would highly recommend him to anyone looking for the best representation in court.

reviews.text: Where do I start with Michael J Mesolella. I first used him back in 2014 when I was in a car accident. After my first meeting with Mike I knew he was someone I would want to keep in my corner, no matter the situation. I have used lawyers in the past but none have compared to the service, compassion, and the feeling of worth that Mike has for his clients. He always answers the phone, and if he is unavailable he is immediate to respond and let me know that he will get back to me as soon as possible. I have never had to wait longer than a day to get a response. He is thorough and takes his time with the cases I have hired him for. He doesn’t rush it or look at me as a “client” or a “number”. He looks at me like a person who entrusted him to get the job done and that is exactly what he has done each and every time. He values and cares for his clients and will fight to the very end until I am completely satisfied and happy with the end result. I am so fortunate to have been able to have him on my side and will continue to hire him and refer him whenever I have the opportunity. I am grateful for his time and dedication and I guarantee you will never find another lawyer who puts their clients above all else! Thank you Michael J. Mesolella and Mesolella and Associates.

I first consulted with Attorney Conlon after nearly a year of representation by another lawyer during a high-conflict divorce with repeated mediation failures and ongoing custody issues. From the very first meeting, I felt an immediate sense of relief. He had reviewed my court documents and case materials in advance and came fully prepared. Our consultation was focused, thoughtful, and thorough—he used the time to understand the full complexity of my situation without any pressure or expectation that I retain him.

He provided practical guidance on how I could proceed even if I chose to stay with my former attorney, and he was candid about how he would approach the case if I moved forward with him. It was refreshing to work with someone willing to dive into the details, validate my experience, offer honest and direct insight into my missteps, and begin building a strategic path forward.

I never doubted his ability to handle my case. Still, the decision to change attorneys after such an emotionally exhausting year was significant, so I returned for a second consultation. That conversation gave me the clarity and confidence I needed to move ahead.

Hiring Attorney Conlon was the best decision I could have made. His representation was exceptional. He has the knowledge, skill, and strategic ability to navigate complicated moving parts and bring focus to what truly matters. Under his leadership, we were able to identify the essential issues and reach a settlement far more favorable than what had previously been possible.

His associate attorneys, Julia Nasiek and Laura Nicholson, are outstanding—professional, prepared, and highly competent. His legal assistants are organized, efficient, and sensitive to the emotional toll of the process.

Throughout all of my work with Attorney Conlon, I never had to worry or second-guess anything. His consistency, integrity, and clear follow-through gave me a level of peace of mind I hadn’t felt in a very long time. That stability allowed me to focus on my children, my career, my wellness, and rebuilding my life.

I am very grateful for the exemplary leadership, successful resolution, and valuable lessons gained from the experience of working with Attorney Conlon.

There is an art to practicing family law at a truly high level—one that requires skill, strategy, organization, patience, emotional intelligence, humility, and unwavering dedication. This is exactly what you will find with Attorney Conlon. I cannot recommend him highly enough for any family law matter.

I can't recommend Attorney Sabra enough. He and his office do incredible work and I couldn't be more thankful for them. I called other attorneys prior to speaking with Sabra Law and was told my case was too small, or the circumstances were too complicated. But when I contacted Sabra, I was helped immediately, I had an appointment within days to go over my case with him. Mr. Sabra never told me that my case was too small or too complicated, even though it was indeed very complicated. He worked on this for more time than I think he bargained for but I never felt like less of a priority, I never felt like I was being overlooked like a big firm would, and he fought for my best interests until the very end. My case settled for more than I had imagined, but more than that, when he told me what the other side's offer was, I told him that was good enough for me but then the next day, he called to tell me he got them to settle for more! I never even asked, never indicated that I wasn't happy with the original offer, he is truly just dedicated to the best possible outcome for his clients. If there is a fight to be had, he will fight for you.

In addition to Mr. Sabra being a great attorney, he has a great staff to back him up. They are always on the ball! I don't think I ever waited for anything. They are prompt, courteous, and it's always the same smiling faces when you walk into their office. I was very nervous about seeking an attorney for my workers comp case but Sabra Law made it easy, my fears were gone after my first visit to their office. They are all such caring understanding, patient, and caring people. I hope and pray that I never find myself in the same position but if I do, Attorney Sabra will be the first person I call.

Truly, call Sabra Law, he is looking out for your best interest!

My wife and I stopped at a yield sign to let traffic pass when another car slammed into our back bumper, causing whiplash injuries, primarily to my wife. She spent several hours in the hospital, and needed follow up care. The damage to my rear bumper looked minimal but turned out to be over $3500. We didn't think we had much of a claim against the other driver, but a family friend told us to call Steven Sabra Law Office in Somerset MA. After a short consultation with Mr. Sabra, and his very professional assistant, DEE we felt assured our case was in good hands. However, we could not imagine how good the Sabra law office would be in this claim. Both Mr. Sabra and DEE were extremely professional. and proficient in rendering us a settlement well beyond our expectations. Should such an unfortunate event happen to anyone else, the Sabra Law Office has our highest recommendation.

WJB, Oct 3 2024

If I could give Negative Stars, I would. Marc Gursky is the epitome of a sleazy lawyer - that works for Unions no less. He was (is) on my charge case (via local 328 against RISE dispensary in Warwick), and did the paperwork & filing for said charge, however when I noticed there could be numerous conflicts of interest, he quickly said he would be happy to withdraw my charge so that I could file one myself. He then also referred me to ask any/all further legal questions to the Union Rep, Miguel Santos, who already proved himself to be a legal liability by misrepresenting Mr. Gursky's position at the start of the issue, as well as not being an attorney.

Now, a month + later, I have filed my own charge with the NLRB, and Mr. Gursky & Union have NOT dropped my charge. Instead, they are continuing on using my charge as a jumping off point for them, while calling me a "liability" to the union and ignoring all communications from me.

Furthermore, I filed the formal complaint with the RI Judiciary Disciplinary Council, and GET THIS: not only can they not reprimand him since he says he's not MY lawyer (but the unions, and yet he's still using MY charge as the basis of his union issue, essentially handling MY case), AND he does lectures for the RI Bar Association- Imagine That; Practically on the payroll for the entity that SHOULD be keeping the judiciary in check, and yet......

If you're looking for an attorney that does the minimum, will mislead you at every turn, NOT do due-diligence on collecting information from imperative witnesses, AND will refer you to non-attorneys for unauthorized practice of law, Marc Gursky's your guy! Plus, the added bonus of having a state rep as a partner, which I'm sure makes him think he's bulletproof. Would only wish his "litigational prowess" on my worst enemies!!

The renovation properties often come with complicated titles (foreclosures, probate, etc) and not once have we worried that it'd turn out ok on the other end. Tony and his team have always gone above and beyond in their roles to make the purchases (and eventual sales) of these properties as quick and easy as possible, while also investigating each property thoroughly.

In addition to real estate, we've used the office for our personal wills/trusts. We found the process to be surprisingly easy to navigate with Anthony's help. It's wild how talking about our kids future after our potential deaths was somehow a pleasant experience. He had so many great points that I never would have considered without his leadership. It was so worth going through this process so that our family, friends and loved ones do not have to guess about any of our wishes, or deal with any legal drama.

The office staff is incredibly friendly and welcoming, responsive and professional. Honestly I cannot say enough good things about this entire firm.

The denial letter again cited my age, yet key medical evidence was missing from my file. That lack of follow-through raises serious concerns about how my case was handled.

From my experience, at no point did I feel truly guided or represented. Navigating an SSDI case is stressful enough, and I expected far more involvement and communication.

I had a terrible experience with Green & Greenberg. I suffer from a serious traumatic brain injury (TBI) and trusted this firm to properly handle my Social Security Disability case. They failed me twice.
There was a serious lack of communication throughout my case. I was not kept informed about important developments and often felt completely in the dark about what was happening with my own claim.
Attorney Joanne did a horrible job representing me at trial. I was contacted only the day before my hearing. There was no meaningful preparation, no proper review of my medical evidence with me, and no clear explanation of what to expect.
Her opening and closing statements were very poor and showed a lack of effort and advocacy.
Because of the lack of communication, preparation, and diligence, my disability claim was denied for my serious medical condition. This firm did not fight for me the way they should have.
I would not recommend Green & Greenberg or Attorney Joanne to anyone seeking disability representation, especially someone with a traumatic brain injury who needs clear communication and strong legal advocacy.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Lea,

Thank you for taking the time to share your concerns. I'm the attorney who represented you at the hearing. I was surprised to read your review, so I went back and took a look at my notes from your 2023 hearing. I always call clients 1-2 days before the hearing to prepare. I apologize if the prep so close to the hearing upset you — I do this so that everything we go over to prepare for the hearing is fresh in clients' minds.
During our hearing prep call, we spent over an hour on the phone. First, I explained step by step what happens during the hearing. Then, we went through hearing prep questions that mimic the questions you get asked on the hearing day. At that time, we reviewed your file and any notes I had, the medical sources we had in your file and any records that we may have been waiting on — which in your case, your file was complete going into the hearing.
I reviewed the hearing as well. I gave an opening statement which stated my legal argument in favor of disability and how that argument was supported. I also did a thorough cross examination of the vocational expert. I did not give a closing statement, as I did not want to repeat myself and anything that needed to come forward in your testimony was addressed through questions I asked.
Unfortunately, the judge that you had at the hearing has a low approval rating. For that reason alone, I wasn't surprised when it was unfavorable, and that is why we appealed. We brought your case as far as we could. While we can't win every case unfortunately, we did fight at the Appeals Council and in Federal District Court to try and get a remand. When you received the denial, I made sure to chat with you for about an hour once again and explain everything that happened and the legal argument that the judge used in denying your claim.
We contacted you both when the Appeals Council sent their denial and after the Federal District Court denial about reapplication. You declined after the AC denial. When your district court denial came in, I directed you to both our firm in RI and a firm in CA if you moved to start a new application. Our initial application team was unable to reach you via phone or mail response after sending paperwork over.
I understand how frustrating this process is and know that it is a lengthy amount of time for things to happen in these courts. However, as your attorney and advocate, I was well prepared for your case and did everything in my power to get a favorable decision. We can't promise to win every case, but the firm and myself have many positive reviews and continue to work towards getting our clients benefits.

Thank you so much for this thoughtful review and for trusting me during such a challenging time in your life. Divorce is never easy, and I’m truly grateful to hear that you felt supported, informed, and advocated for throughout the process.

It means a great deal to know that my approach — staying calm, strategic, and focused on your goals — helped bring some stability to a difficult situation. I appreciate your kind words and your gratitude more than I can say. I wish you continued strength and the very best as you move forward into this next chapter.

reviews.text: I hired Damon as my divorce attorney when it became clear to me that my ex was planning to take advantage of my willingness to resolve things amicably to the fullest extent - at great cost to me. My previous attorney was great, but very focused on wrapping things up as quickly as possible. After reviewing the details of my case, Damon assured me I was in a strong position to secure a settlement that was more beneficial to me and my children. He doesn’t waste your time and money on drafting proposal after proposal like my ex’s attorney did. It was clear to see this was a strategy to charge him for as many billable hours as possible. Damon works smarter, not harder, but does NOT shy away from a fight. He was ready to go to trial if my ex was not willing to relent on some key issues. Damon gave me the confidence and strength I needed to get through my divorce with a settlement that I am satisfied with.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you so much for taking the time to share this, and for trusting me during such an important and difficult moment in your life. What you described is something I see far too often, and I’m glad you reached out when you did.

From the beginning, my priority was making sure you — and especially your children — were protected and treated fairly. I truly appreciate your recognition of the strategy and intention behind how I approach cases: being thoughtful with time and resources, while still standing firm and prepared to fight when it matters most. Knowing that you felt confident, supported, and empowered throughout the process means more to me than I can say.

I’m grateful I was able to help you reach a resolution you can stand behind, and I admire the strength you showed along the way. I wish you and your children nothing but stability, peace, and success in this next chapter.

reviews.ownerResponse:

Clear communication and compassion are priorities for me, especially in situations that impact families so personally. Unfortunately we are ethically bound to not disclose certain details of your case and it's unfortunate you felt the need to provide us with a one star review after everything we attempted to do for you, despite your failure to ever pay your bill as promised. reviews.text: I would like to sure the details of my experience with attorney Dan Marran at PSH. I have been a client of PSH for almost ten years. Dan is a new attorney who came highly recommended from my colleague and classmate at Bishop Hendricken, Joshua Xavier. I also have several other attorneys at PSH handling other cases for me without issue. Dan was an excellent attorney in the beginning. He was very attentive, excellent at reading contract law and interpreting it. I had been working with Dan since June 2024 on a very complex case. We maintained daily email, text and phone dialogue during this entire period. On the night of Monday September 16 I was rushed to the hospital with symptoms of a heart attack. I was kept in the hospital until Thursday morning. During that time, Dan dropped me because it was “untenable” to represent a client with whom he had “no communication.” Keep in mind, if you have an emergency, hospitalization or death in the family with PSH they will not consider that. After four months of daily communication you can be dropped for 48 hours of no contact. Furthermore, upon contacting Dan when I left the hospital there was nothing pressing that needed to be addressed in the 48 hours. Opposing counsel had not returned our previous email, corporate was taking over some projects and we had a pre conference call which we had already discussed ad nauseam. If PSH no longer wanted to rep the case they should have said that instead of making up a weak excuse. I suggest that Dan takes some time to self reflect on his values as a man and a Christian. That is not the way to make partner. God bless you and I wish you the best.

Andrew speaks 3 languages fluently - English, Italian, and Spanish - and can also speak Portuguese.

He is a great resource for referrals in the community - from lenders to insurance providers to mechanics to local political figures, etc, etc. He knows everybody.

Very disappointing interaction with this law firm. Had a potential deal in place to purchase a portfolio from somebody that this law firm represented. Both deals did fall through, not this law firm’s fault. After the first deal went through, sent them an email with my address asking for my deposit back, there was no issue in getting my deposit back from the purchase and sale agreement as they sent it back via US postal service promptly. Second deal that fell through required 14 emails to them with a release that the seller’s agents sent me that specifically asked how I would like my deposit back, I check off via mail and provided my address where requested. Waited a week and still nothing. I finally received this long email about the delays and not going through the proper channels at his firm, blah, blah, blah. The last paragraph finally said something along the lines that for security purposes they do not mail checks and that I was going to have to pick it up at his office knowing that I live out of state. Wait, you just mailed me a check for the first deal that fell through like 2-3 months ago, was there a company policy change? No response when asked. I finally made the trek down to his office where there was no sign of his business, just what appeared to be a common door entrance for the address provided where I thought I might find a directory or a door or sign leading to his office; nope. As soon as I entered there was a open door with approx 5 people huddled around a desk, I was approached by a woman that asked who I was looking for. I explained why I was there, and was told to hold on. 1-2 gentlemen huddled around the round table scurried into an office and said it would need to be cut and hold on a minute. You mean it isn’t ready after you told me to stop by last week to pick it up. After a few minutes, an approx 6 year old girl came out of the office with my check and handed it to me-quite the security procedures you claim to have in place. Something seemed off about this encounter at their office and very disappointing to have to travel to get my check even though you will accept deposit checks via mail and have sent them back via mail, also should not have taken 14 emails to get this straightened out. When I was asked to send a check after signing a P&S both times I did so the next day. Be professional!

If you ever find yourself in a situation where you need someone to talk to regarding any personal injury, please reach out to Dennis and his team at Teravainen Law. After a car accident I found myself frustrated as my life had been turned upside down due to a head on collision. A family and two kids, I was unfairly put in a position where I was injured with potential medical bills amounting in the tens of thousands. Within 24 hours of contacting Dennis, I was on the phone with him explaining my situation. He listened, he was empathetic and understanding. His

My Experience Working with Bill Kolb
Finding the Right Advocate
About three years ago, in 2023, a friend who worked in the Rhode Island court system recommended Bill Kolb to me. I was told he was knowledgeable, honest, and conscientious. My own experiences with Bill validated this recommendation. Before contacting Bill, I reached out to several other attorneys. Each one told me that pursuing the criminal who had caused so much pain in our lives was a waste of time, and they did not want to get involved.
Dedication and Professionalism
In contrast, Bill always took my calls, and the amount of time he spent in court, preparing, and working on the case was impressive. His commitment was unwavering.
Results Achieved
After three years of hard work, patience, and persistence, Bill's efforts paid off. Last night, he hand-delivered a check for the money that had been stolen from us, along with interest. Bill Kolb exemplifies the ideal attorney often depicted in television and movies—he is the real deal.

For the past year, Bill helped me navigate through a profoundly annoying situation with my former building firm and their equally mendacious lawyer. Litigating the details of a property lien is a topic that very few lawyers are interested in dealing with. I was turned down or outright ignored by five other firms before receiving a callback from Bill who agreed to take on my case. He was kind, courteous, and generous in his willingness to hear what I had to say. During negotiations, he was professional, patient and practical about what our outcomes would be. We were able to avoid a potentially nasty court fight, and for that I am very grateful. I highly recommend this law firm for their services. If ever I need real estate legal help again, they are first on my call list.

reviews.text: Only had a phone call and a ton-TON- of ton of tonedeaf txts from "Thierry" that i eventually had to block. Have had debilitating health issues for years, and got a new disease ( sarcoidosis) and pacemaker/ ICD in feb 25, that has made my day to day life extremely difficult. My therapist suggested looking into disability- I got a call/ voicemail orginally from a male lawyer, I called the number he gave in the voicemail and was met with the most rude secretary, who was so callous, anyways she told me i had to call the local SS office to find out my date last insured. I call the SS office and the person I talked to was more helpful but told me that finding out the date last insured is something the law firm should be doing and not me. After that, they kept calling everyday-EVERYDAY- and not leaving messages, and they never emailed me, which is what i orginally requested to avoid situations like this that DO NOT HELP MY HEALTH!! Thanks- theres more lawyers out there- avoid this law firm at all cost!

Before I signed with this firm, they called me every day, emailed me, and texted me. They said, "We're going to work on your case. We'll consult with our partner to see if there's a conflict of interest." They evaluated and reviewed my case. Then, total silence for about two months. They contacted me, but they haven't received my records. I'm a patient who is allergic to chlorine. I've had allergy tests that have come back positive since I was a child. Now, I can't have chlorine in my house. I have to bathe with special filters; I can't swim in a pool. At the Rhode Island hospital, on more than one occasion—around five times—they used chlorine on me, which led to me being intubated for several weeks. Not once or twice, but five times. I was intubated because of what I did. Their protocol included a sign saying I was allergic to [unclear - possibly "Breathe-Schung" or "Sn" in some other contexts]. And yet, I kept going back. I contacted this law firm, and they said, "We're going to take your case. We have your record." For four months, I had absolutely no communication with the law firm. Nothing. They said they were reviewing my case. The last thing they told me was that I would contact partner Mari, the notary, and give you an answer in a week. They said, "Give me a week to work on getting back to you about what we're going to do." After that week, they contacted me via email with a simple "they can't take my case," saying that if we don't go, we have to prove I'm allergic. But the allergy test is right there, the evidence of how many times I was intubated, the doctors' written records explaining why I was intubated. That was it. So I started investigating, looking into the hospital's lawyer, and with the people I had met with at the beginning, the hospital's representatives happen to be study partners of several lawyers at this firm, which is kind of strange, I think. So I had to go to Boston to have the case evaluated there to take action because it seems odd that for more than four months they tell me, "Yes, we'll work at your house," and then suddenly overnight, "Oh, we can't work with you." In this case, they haven't even wanted to return my record, nothing. They've left me in limbo, like they're trying to stab me in the back, not knowing that I could be hospitalized for several months, more than three months in the hospital. Now I need to use oxygen, I can't work. All of this is happening at home. I'm 36 years old, I have two daughters. I didn't ask to be unable to work. I went to a hospital to seek help. I went to a hospital because I had a condition, where it's supposed to be the safest place. The Rhode Island hospital put my life at risk more than five times. I can't work because of the oxygen deficiency in my lungs, the damage caused by so many intubations. My body has been severely affected, and this law firm tells me it's difficult because if we go to court, we have to find an expert who can prove that, and it's very, very difficult to prove. Meanwhile, I have to suffer like this, and they call themselves fighting for the health of patients.

We are focused on analyzing your case and welcome the opportunity to continue addressing any questions directly.
