reviews.text: I have to say personally.She is the best if you're Looking for someone that's gonna go with the whole 15 rounds Joyce, is the person excellent lawyer?
I couldn't have asked for a more knowledgeable, courteous, and empathetic attorney to represent me.

I hired Deb Tate to represent me in my divorce, a decision I regret to this day due to her shoddy, lazy, negligent lawyering.

I was married for 14 years. My husband was a CFO and professional negotiator who represented himself. I have an MBA and an MPA. He insisted that I become a stay-at-home mom for our daughter and family.

I did absolutely everything – a hot home-cooked meal every night, housework, laundry, childcare, non-stop trad wife. I hardly slept and never had a break, even when I was sick or injured. I also had a side gig as a nonprofit consultant because I wanted to contribute to our household income.  I gave 200% to my marriage and kept all of my marriage vows. While I was not perfect (who is?) I did everything I could to nurture and save my marriage.

When I asked to end it, all I wanted was a fair settlement and no drama. I wasn’t interested in revenge or taking him to the cleaners, though I could have. He convinced me that we could mediate a settlement between ourselves. I was physically and emotionally exhausted and mentally fragile. It seemed reasonable since I didn’t want to waste a lot of money on legal fees and I wanted to protect my daughter from suffering.

Here is the deal I presented to Deb Tate: No alimony, no child support, an equity payout on our house (below FMV) so he would keep the house, no financial disclosure, 50/50 split of assets in Vanguard, I would receive ½ of 1/14 of his 401k, and I would be responsible for half of our child’s private school tuition, half of her college expenses, and half of her other expenses. He claimed that he did not put any money into a 401k during our marriage outside of the first year so there were no other 401k assets.

We would share 50/50 custody where our daughter would spend Sunday night through Thursday morning with me and Thursday afternoon through Sunday evening with him. (I know that’s not 50/50.)

Deb said nothing except to question the placement schedule and grilled me and billed me for an hour of her time on that topic without providing helpful guidance.

She did not tell me to counteroffer on the deal. She did not express any shock, surprise, or concern that I was being manipulated or lied to.

She did not warn me of the long-term consequences of no financial disclosure, and how he could hide money and assets and he would never be accountable. She did not question the value of our house in a tony zip code.

She never told me that child support is always open and if my income changed by more than 10%, I could apply for it. Deb did not tell me what I was legally entitled to under RI law, did not discuss or question any assets, did not say anything about the fact that I lost 14 years of income and retirement contributions during peak earning years as a high-capacity earner.

She did not question the apples-to-oranges comparison of his income, retirement, and job security as a fully-loaded C-Suite executive and mine as a nonprofit freelancer with highly variable income.

I estimate that my ex-husband is worth at least $4M-$5M. I ended up with $750k in the divorce. I even had a post-nup that was drawn up after our first separation early in our marriage. Deb Tate didn’t use it.

I was too weak to think clearly and I relied on Deb to be the excellent representation I needed to advocate for me and my daughter. She did NOTHING. She just processed the paperwork and charged me hefty fees to do it.

I would have been better off with Legal Aid because at least they would have cared.

While my experience was terrible, I hope that I have prevented you from making my mistake. Deb Tate is a pathetic excuse for a divorce attorney, unless you are the other spouse. My ex-husband was thrilled.

Ms. Laura Ruzzo Reale previously represented my family.
However, she misused confidential information obtained during that representation to her own advantage in a subsequent legal matter involving my wife.
Specifically, Ms. Reale used this information to gain an unfair advantage against me in a restraining order/divorce proceeding where she represented my wife.
This conduct demonstrates a concerning prioritization of personal gain over the best interests of the children involved, which is a fundamental ethical obligation for any attorney practicing family law.
I possess documentation to support these allegations.

Absolutely worst law office I have ever dealt with. Got an attitude and told me to notify when I got a court date. Then dropped me because of to much of a backlog of clients. After I paid the consultation fee of course.

My experience a few years ago was much like Lorie's. Laura repeatedly lied to my face and charged an arm and a leg. She also repeatedly forgot the details of my case and I ended up paying and owing thousands and thousands. At the end they billed me under an incorrect name when they knew my current one. They also charge interest for balances due. She misunderstood very important details and didn't clarify them until paperwork was signed around those details. Never, ever hiring them again and absolutely do not recommend. It's one thing to lose a case. That's out of a lawyer's direct control. It's another to handle it that badly.

I had Laura Ruzzo Reale as my attorney in a divorce case. I spent almost $30k , she did nothing for me that a mediator couldn't have done. She was very unprofessional in the way she talked to me as well as comments she made about the other attorney in the case.
She put a lien on my house without even contacting me when I had already gave her almost $20k in cash, she knew I wasn't going to have access to any other cash until the divorce was final. She required a $5k retainer, when she thought we we're going to trial , she wanted another $10k to start working on preparation for trial, then before we went to trial she wanted the balance of her bill paid off. We never made it to trial, I had to settle because I couldn't continue to pay her for not working on my behalf. I had to ask her to withdraw as my counsel because she was not helping me and charging me for time and hours that I know we're not accurate.
Been reaching out to the wrongful prosecutors. No responses or cooperation over months. Legal negligence among other offenses committed.

I have reached out through emails and nothing. I requested what card is this due to because i just paid off 5 cards in two weeks. Recieved a letter to go to court over ganishment, i will take the sick day and show up

Everybody is having the same experience I am having. The legal assistant is nothing other than rude, ignorant and says she can't give certain advice on the phone because she's not the attorney however she tries acting like the attorney on the phone. This entire firm is crooked. No wonder people have negative reviews on this place. What a joke. I tried getting information on how I go about paying my judgement and all I got was "well you just pay it and thats it" I feel like its unbeknownst to them that you can make a payment plan towards this... To top it off i reached out over 2 years ago for a settlement offer and it took them up until i sent a "friendly" email 2 weeks ago to get a phone call and getting badgered by the legal assistant on the phone telling me its my fault and that I had all this time to do something and even though I reached out 2 years ago she defends the client by saying they just didn't respond until now and they dont want to settle.... 1 week before I have to turn in a form so my wages don't get garnished HOW CONVEINENT ......
They were extremely rude. They never called me back with any updates.

Horrible customer service from Nichole Duwhatever! She is very rude & pushy to clients and tried to force me to still speak with her after she got loud and nasty with me over the phone. There are some very sweet people that work here but she is a disaster waiting to happen! I even had my grandmother call and she told the other people in the office to direct our calls right to her.

I’ve been working with this office to resolve an outstanding debt, but my experience has been extremely disappointing. I have been on multiple calls trying to get this resolved promptly.

Most recently, after submitting documentation and an asset form that was described to me as urgent— on 11/10—I received no confirmation via email or phone. I followed up on 11/27, only to be told they never received my email. While on the call, I resent the email, and it was finally acknowledged. However, throughout my interactions, I felt like the people who are supporting this case have been snappy and unkind.

When I expressed my concerns about being treated poorly and receiving misinformation by one legal assistant, I was met with defensiveness rather than an apology for my experience. The woman I was on the call with then shifted her tone and stated they didn’t “appreciate” me singling out her colleague and claimed it was documented "the other way around." I was baffled by the defensiveness, and it seemed culturally this firm operates this way.

Throughout calls with this firm, I have been anxious, asking questions for clarification and to ensure I had the correct expectations, as I want to resolve this issue quickly. However, I’ve consistently been dismissed, treated rudely, and felt forced to change my tone to be firmer to ensure I’m given accurate information and can move things along.

I was further told that defendants need to be accountable for following up on their cases, which felt dismissive given their lack of communication. The experience left me feeling unheard and gaslit.

Even on debt-related calls, it’s essential for representatives to interact with kindness and professionalism. People in these situations are often already feeling vulnerable or stressed, and the tone and approach of those handling the calls can make a significant difference. The lack of empathy and accountability I experienced here is unacceptable. As someone with experience in Legal and Customer Service, I find it bizarre for people in these positions to act this way.

Looking at the other reviews, it appears this is a pattern of behavior. It seems this firm does not care about their reputation or firm identity. Hopefully, I can get this resolved soon and move on.
Stop harassing the wrong people!!
Tom Lyons was professional and listened to my concerns. I get the sense the other party doesn’t want to deal with him… Sorry for the late review and thank you very much for your help!!!!!

My father-in-law and I interviewed 4 attorneys before hiring Craig. He wasn't the most expensive and he wasn't the least expensive, but he was the best. From our initial consultation, it was clear that we were not just paying for another lawyer; we were investing in Craig's expertise and experience.
I was confident in Craig's ability to defend my husband. He took the time to listen and meticulously investigate every detail of the case in order to get all of the facts correct. I also was glad that he had experience as a prosecutor before becoming a criminal defense attorney. As a result, he was familiar with the DA's approach and was able to adequately prepare for the case.
My husband's case went to trial and he was found not guilty on all charges. Craig was impressive in the courtroom; well-prepared, organized and a skillful interrogator. I always felt my husband and I were in capable hands.
If you are facing any charges and need the best defense possible, Craig is the attorney you need to hire. When the stakes are terrifyingly high, do not compromise. He is worth every penny and one of the best investments we made.

Jude kerrison handled my workers comp case around 2010.  When we went to court he wasn’t familiar enough with the details of my injury and barely put up any argument against the company lawyer. Before our next court date he came to me with a settlement offer. I was to get 600 dollars and he was getting 2000 for his fee. When I refused it he got upset and his voice went up like a little girl. He asked “ how am I supposed to get paid?” I said do your job and bring me a real offer. He said don’t bother showing up at court because he was going to tell judge we were dropping claim. Then hung up on me. My case was against 2 companies. One in Rhode Isl one in Massachusetts. The judge in Rhode Island ruled in my favor for 67500 dollars. He said make sure my lawyer holds company in Massachusetts accountable as both were liable for the injury. Kerrison got me a offer of 600 dollars vs the 67500 from Rhode Island?  And he would get 2000? I wouldn’t let that guy represent me on a parking ticket. Mishandled my case and cost thousands and thousands of dollars.  My doctor said I got hurt at work. Company doctor said I got hurt at work. Independent state doctor said I got hurt at work. How bad of a lawyer do you have to be to only get 600 dollar offer?  Pathetic.

My husband and I hired Attorney Megan from Sheehan & Associates to represent him during a complaint process with the RI Department of Business Regulation, and the experience exceeded every expectation we had. From the very beginning, Megan was incredibly attentive, knowledgeable, and thorough. She took the time to answer every question we had, explained each step of the process clearly, and made us feel genuinely supported throughout the entire journey.

What stood out most was not just her intelligence and sharp legal skills, but the empathy she brought to our case. It is rare to find an attorney who balances compassion with exceptional professionalism, but Megan does it flawlessly. Her passion for representing her clients was evident in the way she prepared, communicated, and advocated on our behalf.

The entire staff at Sheehan & Associates deserves recognition as well. Every interaction was polite, respectful, and timely. This is one of the few law offices that consistently returns calls, follows up without being asked, and treats clients like real people—not case numbers.

We were extremely satisfied with the outcome of our case, and because of the outstanding service and dedication this firm demonstrated, we have hired them full-time to handle all of our legal matters moving forward. Their level of commitment, expertise, and integrity is unmatched.

I would highly recommend Sheehan & Associates to anyone seeking top-tier legal representation. They truly are a firm you can trust with confidence.

I am extremely grateful for the outstanding legal representation provided by Attorney Adam Sloane. I was facing serious and completely false accusations, and Mr. Sloane made himself available on very short notice—approximately three business days before the hearing—and offered his services at a reasonable and fair price.

From the very beginning, he was professional, attentive, kind, and highly knowledgeable. He took the time to truly listen to me, understand the details of my situation, and guide me step by step through the legal process. His preparation was exceptional. He thoroughly prepared me for both the hearing and the interrogation process, ensuring the right questions were asked to uncover critical information related to the other party’s motives.

Despite the limited time to prepare, the outcome was successful, and I genuinely felt defended and supported throughout the entire process. As a military service member, being subjected to false and cruel allegations was incredibly stressful, but Mr. Sloane handled my case with care, integrity, and determination. Justice was served.

Attorney Adam Sloane is someone who truly cares about his clients and fights for them. Without his representation, the outcome could have been very different and unjust. He was truly a Godsend, and I highly recommend him to anyone in need of strong, ethical, and compassionate legal defense.

reviews.text:
I worked with Colleen Hollis through a very difficult divorce, and I’m grateful to have had her in my corner. She was respectful of my retainer and never pushed unnecessary actions just to inflate my bill. Instead, she consistently offered thoughtful options at each fork in the road, even when things were emotionally charged or legally complex.

What stood out most was her steady, no-nonsense approach. She didn’t sugarcoat things to appease me, but instead kept my expectations realistic while always advocating in my best interest. Even when opposing counsel tried aggressive or theatrical tactics, she remained completely unfazed and focused, which gave me confidence throughout the process.

If you’re looking for someone solid, strategic, and mindful with both your time and money, I’d absolutely recommend!

I hired attorney Colleen Hollis from this law firm to just write a letter to my sons school. In the past any other law firm to write a letter would charge a flat fee between $250 and $500. This attorney wanted a retainer for a letter of $2500. Now mind you I paid this because I was in a time sensitive situation. It was all downhill from here. The attorney wrote a letter that didn't get me anywhere. I actually fixed this all on my own. The attorney would not answer my phone calls or emails until a week after I contacted the office which is unacceptable. I asked for a portion of the retainer back and ironically the bill was exactly $2500 for a letter. So no communication, not responsive, only wrote one letter and I get charged $2500. I fixed this all on my own with zero help from the attorney. This attorney just cares about money and nothing else didn't get the job done and severely overcharged for a letter. I got the job done myself and my son finished the school year out and graduated. Run far away from this law firm. I have actually filed a formal complaint with the Mass Bar Association and will be contacting other firms as well to recover a portion of my retainer back.

reviews.ownerResponse: Mrs. Carvalho: First off, we are truly sorry for your loss. However, we have no record of you or your husband contacting our office for any matter. If in fact we have your mother-in-law's Will, please feel free to call our office at 508-567-0484 in order that we may assist you. Again, we are truly sorry for your loss.

reviews.text: Only had a phone call and a ton-TON- of ton of tonedeaf txts from "Thierry" that i eventually had to block. Have had debilitating health issues for years, and got a new disease ( sarcoidosis) and pacemaker/ ICD in feb 25, that has made my day to day life extremely difficult. My therapist suggested looking into disability- I got a call/ voicemail orginally from a male lawyer, I called the number he gave in the voicemail and was met with the most rude secretary, who was so callous, anyways she told me i had to call the local SS office to find out my date last insured. I call the SS office and the person I talked to was more helpful but told me that finding out the date last insured is something the law firm should be doing and not me. After that, they kept calling everyday-EVERYDAY- and not leaving messages, and they never emailed me, which is what i orginally requested to avoid situations like this that DO NOT HELP MY HEALTH!! Thanks- theres more lawyers out there- avoid this law firm at all cost!

Before I signed with this firm, they called me every day, emailed me, and texted me. They said, "We're going to work on your case. We'll consult with our partner to see if there's a conflict of interest." They evaluated and reviewed my case. Then, total silence for about two months. They contacted me, but they haven't received my records. I'm a patient who is allergic to chlorine. I've had allergy tests that have come back positive since I was a child. Now, I can't have chlorine in my house. I have to bathe with special filters; I can't swim in a pool. At the Rhode Island hospital, on more than one occasion—around five times—they used chlorine on me, which led to me being intubated for several weeks. Not once or twice, but five times. I was intubated because of what I did. Their protocol included a sign saying I was allergic to [unclear - possibly "Breathe-Schung" or "Sn" in some other contexts]. And yet, I kept going back. I contacted this law firm, and they said, "We're going to take your case. We have your record." For four months, I had absolutely no communication with the law firm. Nothing. They said they were reviewing my case. The last thing they told me was that I would contact partner Mari, the notary, and give you an answer in a week. They said, "Give me a week to work on getting back to you about what we're going to do." After that week, they contacted me via email with a simple "they can't take my case," saying that if we don't go, we have to prove I'm allergic. But the allergy test is right there, the evidence of how many times I was intubated, the doctors' written records explaining why I was intubated. That was it. So I started investigating, looking into the hospital's lawyer, and with the people I had met with at the beginning, the hospital's representatives happen to be study partners of several lawyers at this firm, which is kind of strange, I think. So I had to go to Boston to have the case evaluated there to take action because it seems odd that for more than four months they tell me, "Yes, we'll work at your house," and then suddenly overnight, "Oh, we can't work with you." In this case, they haven't even wanted to return my record, nothing. They've left me in limbo, like they're trying to stab me in the back, not knowing that I could be hospitalized for several months, more than three months in the hospital. Now I need to use oxygen, I can't work. All of this is happening at home. I'm 36 years old, I have two daughters. I didn't ask to be unable to work. I went to a hospital to seek help. I went to a hospital because I had a condition, where it's supposed to be the safest place. The Rhode Island hospital put my life at risk more than five times. I can't work because of the oxygen deficiency in my lungs, the damage caused by so many intubations. My body has been severely affected, and this law firm tells me it's difficult because if we go to court, we have to find an expert who can prove that, and it's very, very difficult to prove. Meanwhile, I have to suffer like this, and they call themselves fighting for the health of patients.
reviews.ownerResponse: Natanael, we are disappointed to read this review and take your concerns very seriously. Our firm is committed to professional communication and thoughtful case evaluation. Although we cannot share details publicly, once we became aware of your concerns, a senior partner personally reached out, and the matter is now being actively handled. We are focused on analyzing your case and welcome the opportunity to continue addressing any questions directly.

reviews.text: I contacted this firm to make a consult, then they emailed and texted me everyday until I got back to them.  I was in the hospital throwing up blood for a month straight so I couldn't respond for a 2 weeks from there 1st attempt to contact me.   When I finally responded to their email to set up a appointment, all the texts stopped and emails stopped.  No response still as I write this review.  And I was a former client of this firm.  The woman's name that ghosts me was Mary Silvia.  Extremely disappointed in this firm's attention to get business then choose not to even talk to me anymore.  I dont know why still til today.  I would stay away from a lawyers office that treats actual clients this way. Very bad business.  Ill repeat, emailed and texted everyday for 14 days, then when I responded, they stopped all communication. Never even spoke to anyone at all...ever.  Poor communication, ghosting actual clients.  I'd stay away from this firm if I was you.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear K,

Thank you for taking the time to share your experience. We are truly sorry to hear about the challenges you faced, both personally and in your interactions with our firm. First and foremost, we hope you are feeling better and recovering well after such a difficult time.

We take feedback like yours very seriously, and we deeply regret that you felt let down by our communication. Unfortunately, we have been unable to locate your information in our system to understand what may have happened in this situation. We sincerely apologize for any frustration or inconvenience caused.

We would like the opportunity to make this right and address your concerns. Please reach out to us directly at 401-274-7400 so we can investigate further and ensure this matter is resolved to your satisfaction.

We went to him for help with disability for our daughter. We explained to them that we had looked into it in the past but felt we would need a lawyer since every claim seems to need an appeal.

They were very nice while helping us fill out the initial application. Then they completely ghosted us. Our claim was denied for reasons that weren't event true, so we reached out to the attorney's office. We reached out via email 3 times and we called over 10 times over the course of a month. Every time they told us the denial was on his desk and he'd get right back to us. When it got close to 60 days since our denial, we realized they were just going to ignore us and we had to do the appeal ourselves since you only have 60 days to appeal. We've now been dealing with appeal issues on our own and it's been the headache we thought it would be. We wish we had gone to a lawyer that would've followed through and helped us. We have absolutely no idea why he chose to completely drop us and not help us.

reviews.text:
I was rear ended in a motor vehicle accident just after moving to the area. I love that Ken is a local attorney at a small firm. He was amicable and easy to connect with. He made a great suggestions of where I could go to get care since I was new to the area. He and his team did periodic check-ins that were just right. (I am a teacher and have a side hustle working for Amazon, so I have a very full work life. Therefore it was essential for me to have an attorney that could handle most things without me.) Ken and his secretary Ashley were flexible yet very responsive to all my inquiries and did a phenomenal job of getting things done. At the end of my case Ken offered several clear options I could take, gave me his professional opinion, but did not pressure on me to decide any certain way.

In short, Ken is awesome, he is down to earth and clearly knows his stuff, he is fair, reasonable, and well priced. I highly recommend Amoriggi Law Office. (Figuratively speaking, I almost want something else to happen just so I can work with him again; it was such a pleasant experience. Ken is one of the good guys and could totally fit the prototype of a small town vigilante.) Thank you Ken, Ashley, and Amoriggi Law! You made my motor vehicle accident less painful to deal with.
~Hannah

Sought legal representation for a previous MA/RI order. After briefly explaining my situation to Ms. Achilles assistant over the phone, she recommended an in- person consultation. Upon the consult, I was asked to again to explain in writing, a brief summary of the reiteration on a short form giving my reasons once again for the consult. I then paid the assistant 100.00 up front, to sit with Ms. Achille. She said she would give the summary to Ms. Achille to view and she would be out to greet us shortly. I paid 100.00 to find out this office doesn’t represent Massachusetts family probate court as it says on their website. This law firm is dishonest, and was a complete waste of my time and money. Ms. Achille also seemed to be on her lunch break during our consult, sitting with me while snapping into an apple and drinking her water while she was listening to me. Her “helpful” referrals left me to subpar recommendations.
Angelina Landi at TJC had our case and was a phenomenal lawyer. I couldn't be more thankful for her efforts. Wholeheartedly endorse.

Tim Conlon is at the top of his family law game.  And everyone inside the Rhode Island family law system knows it.

His hard hitting briefs, aggressive depositions, and respected presence in the courts combines with his stellar reputation among judges, guardians, and family evaluators alike to create a force to be reckoned with.

His rates may be higher than others but he more than makes up for it with effective advocacy on behalf of your child.

After several years of playing ping pong with my ex, and three attorneys later, I finally found an attorney who can deliver positive results.  Just a few words to describe this man: professional, concise, and utterly truthful.  I am sincerely grateful for the services and peace of mind that Tim has provided for me and my family.

Very knowledgeable and helpful. Were with me every step of the way. I would highly recommend the firm. Angelina Landi could not have been any more helpful or accommodating. If you are in need of an attorney look no further.

From my perspective divorce has two key components - the emotional side and the financial side.

When leaving my marriage of 23 years,  I sought joy and an ease in my life that was not often available. I entered the divorce proceeding with an initial objective; healing the overwhelming emotions of loss, while moving forward with grace.

And then things got complicated.

I discovered my former husband had hidden marital assets from me for decades.  His near legendary, deceptive strategies during the divorce proceeding required a 2 year battle.

My former husband owns at least 4 businesses, 15 banking/investment accounts and more than 30 parcels of real estate including rental income on most properties. To settle my divorce fairly required an experienced knowledge of commercial banking, real estate finance, and intricate attempts to hide assets.

Within 6 weeks and headed toward a trial, my initial counsel withdrew from my divorce case overnight. Tim Conlon dissected the property settlement agreement proposed by the opposing counsel, noting a significant inequity of $998,000 and agreed to take my case immediately.

I needed to know I had not been taken advantage of by my former husband. TJC.ESQ uses advanced technology for accumulating asset data and calculating the value. I felt included in the process and frequently received education regarding particulars for which I was unfamiliar. The on-line system allowed me to “see” the marital estate. On the first instance of reviewing the marital estate Tim uploaded and projected on wall a spread sheet providing a detailed description of all assets and their values.

To manage the details of the divorce in rapid speed required the additional skills of “people management.” Tim quashed my former husband’s hostile, and inflammatory temperament with ease.  Tim flipped my the extreme domination of the divorce proceeding into a defensive negotiating posture.  In observance of his tactical skill I felt in control of the outcome of my divorce for the for the first time since initiating the dissolve of my marriage.

Based on the firm’s ability to manage critical asset details though technology, Tim’s experience with finance and managing difficult spouses, Tim re-wrote my property settlement and skillfully led my husband to a settlement agreement in clear language for which I could understand and was 1/3 more than proposed by the opposing counsel.

As a survivor of a two year lengthy and highly contested divorce I am grateful Tim Conlon shares my belief  - ‘begin again, don’t look back’ - to do so you can not feel you have been taken advantage and without a fair division of marital assets you may not be able to  activate a plan for rebuilding the joyful life you deserve.

Do not go there, charged me $7500 for a DCYF case which should be very simple, didn't do anything in court and allowed them to convert a hold on my baby to a temporary custody, after a week without answering the phone or emails charged me another $7500 to continue with my case. I end up losing both my children because I wanted to transfer my newborn baby to a different hospital. Do not trust these people!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Daniel, we were very sorry you chose to discharge us before we were able to achieve a successful outcome for your case. We were however up front with you from the beginning about the complicated nature of your case and the time and money involved in resolving it, given the battle with DCYF that was presented. Unfortunately, your characterization of what transpired is not consistent with what actually occurred. You never suggested that there was a question about the time that was documented and billed.

Ultimately we set you up with the services you needed to succeed and gave you a path you needed to follow, and you elected not to follow that path. Again, we were also disappointed with the outcome of the case, and wish him the best in the future.
